warnings: ref_HA: 1 imaginary mode(s) — not a true minimum; pKa is approximate.
ref_A_minus: 1 imaginary mode(s) — not a true minimum; pKa is approximate.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Aqueous acidity (pKa) of phenol via an isodesmic (‘reference’) cycle at the GFN2-xTB level:

Method provenance
• Property: pKa (acid dissociation constant, dimensionless) in water  
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) — no Gaussian basis, no DFT functional  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied single conformers for HA (phenol, charge 0, mult 1) and A⁻ (phenoxide, charge –1, mult 1); likewise for reference acetic acid/acetate. Each species received an xtb micro-optimization + numerical frequency calc (no Boltzmann conformer averaging). Two reference species show one soft (<50 cm⁻¹) imaginary mode each; treated as floppy rotors per chemkit default.  
• Solvent/environment: ALPB implicit water (continuum model, no explicit solvent)  
• Charge/multiplicity: phenol HA 0/1, phenoxide –1/1; acetic acid 0/1, acetate –1/1  
• Software: xtb 6.5 (via chemkit pka-acidity skill)  
• Mode: reference (isodesmic) against acetic acid (experimental pKa = 4.76)  
• Convergence & caveats: all SCFs and optimizations converged. Warnings about soft modes on reference pair (see above). Result is single-conformer; xtb pKa estimates are typically ±2 pKa units.

Headline result
Phenol pKa (water, 298 K, implicit, reference-mode/GFN2-xTB) = 16.6553 (dimensionless)

Reproducibility
Command issued (see live `.out`):
 pka --method xtb --solvent water --mode reference --ha phenol/ha.xyz --a-minus phenol/a_minus.xyz --ref-ha acetic/ha.xyz --ref-a-minus acetic/a_minus.xyz --pka-ref 4.76
The live output log streamed to the working directory during the run contains SCF, optimization, and frequency details.
